name: Mushroom Pate
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
2. Add leek and garlic to the skillet.
3. Sauté until the leek is tender, about 8 minutes.
4. Stir in mushrooms, parsley, and thyme. Sauté until the mushrooms release their juices and begin to brown, about 10 minutes.
5. Stir in white wine or sherry. Season with pepper and salt to taste.
6. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the liquid is reduced and thickened, about 10 minutes.
7. Allow the mixture to cool slightly.
8. Puree the mixture until smooth using a blender or food processor.
9. Spoon the pate into a 3-cup terrine mold.
10. Refrigerate, covered, for several hours or overnight.
11. Garnish with fresh herbs and carrot flowers, if desired.
12. Serve with toasted French bread slices.
13. The intensely-flavored spread can be prepared up to two days in advance and stored tightly wrapped in the refrigerator. If desired, substitute some of the more exotic mushrooms like shiitake, enoki, or oyster for part of the mushrooms.
